author: mark
text:
INPUT FROM bl-d66e (closed 2026-06-01; see bl-2e26 "§ STATE TRANSITIONS"). The sibling delivery/worktree plugin (owns the code worktree) adds rollback cases this topic must cover:

- THIRD side-effect category. This topic frames rollback as "EXTERNAL side effects (tracker push, jira ticket), since discarding the change worktree undoes FILE changes." The delivery plugin adds a middle category: a COMMIT on the PROJECT repo's integration branch (review.post squashes work/<id>→integration). Discarding the change worktree does NOT undo it — that commit lives in a second git repo balls-state core never touches. So three tiers: {change-worktree file changes — auto-undone by discard} vs {project-repo commits — plugin must `git reset`} vs {external services — plugin best-effort}.

- ORDERING RULE for the un-undoable. review.post's squash lands on the integration branch; if a LATER post-plugin fails, rollback must un-squash (local `git reset`, but UN-PUSHABLE once a forge has the branch). bl-d66e's mitigation: the delivery/squash plugin takes the HIGHEST NN- prefix in `review.post/` so nothing runs after it, making its rollback the rare path. Generalize as a contract recommendation: a plugin with an un-undoable side-effect should sort LAST in its phase.

- CROSS-OP state, not just pre→post. The delivery plugin remembers the code-worktree path across THREE ops (claim.post creates, review.post squashes, close.post removes), not only pre→post within one op. The scratch-dir-under-`plugins/<name>/` convention this topic is choosing must also serve id-keyed cross-op persistence, not just same-op pre→post handoff.

author: Mintier
text:
Resolve pre/post rollback semantics into spec §8/§14

Settle bl-b0b6 by editing bl-2e26: rewrite §8 (op lifecycle as balls-authors-base -> pre modifiers -> atomic seal=commit+integrate -> post reactors, ball-immutable), add §14 (the general rollback rule), and de-specialize forge.

Key decisions: three side-effect tiers (ball record/core un-seal; other-local-repo/plugin git reset; external/best-effort); rollback spans the seal boundary (op is the unit of atomicity); best-effort, exit ignored, idempotent; derive-first state, id-keyed scratch only for non-derivable, env cannot cross invocations; un-undoable sorts last.

Forge is no longer a deferred mode: it is the ordinary close plus a claim.post approval gate child (a normal close-blocker), with idempotent push/PR whose rollback no-ops. Persistence-through-abort is a plugin choosing a no-op rollback, not a core carve-out. Added the update verb to §5/§9.
